narrator.personality: Ops lead who set up the alerting migration three weeks ago. Methodical, embarrassed. Speaks about the customer impact in terms of stale data and lost trust.

story_beats.message: The alarm has been in ALARM state for 47 minutes. It fired correctly. The SNS topic received the message. But nobody on the team knew until the tweet.
I found the confirmation email in the ops inbox. It was in the spam folder. Three weeks old. The subject line said 'AWS Notification - Subscription Confirmation.'
Six customers have reported stale dashboards. The data pipeline has been failing for almost an hour. One customer mentioned us in a LinkedIn post about 'vendor reliability.'
The DynamoDB throttling is a real issue, but this simulation is about why nobody was notified. The alarm fired. The question is what happened after that.

glossary.SNS Topic: A communication channel in Amazon Simple Notification Service. When a message is published to a topic, SNS attempts to deliver it to every subscription on that topic.
glossary.Subscription: A connection between an SNS topic and an endpoint (like an email address, phone number, or Lambda function). Each subscription has a protocol and a delivery status.
glossary.PendingConfirmation: The status of an SNS subscription that has been created but not yet verified by the recipient. SNS silently drops all messages to subscriptions in this state -- no error, no log entry.
glossary.CloudWatch Alarm: A rule that watches a metric over time and takes an action (like publishing to an SNS topic) when the metric crosses a threshold you define.
glossary.Dead-Letter Queue: A queue that captures messages or invocations that failed to process. Without one, failed deliveries disappear silently with no record of what went wrong.
glossary.SMS Spending Limit: A monthly dollar cap on SMS messages sent through SNS. The default is $1.00 per month. When the limit is reached, SNS stops sending SMS messages for the rest of the month with no notification.
glossary.NumberOfNotificationsFailed: A CloudWatch metric published by SNS that counts how many delivery attempts failed for a topic. Monitoring this metric is the primary way to detect a broken notification pipeline.
glossary.Webhook: An HTTP endpoint that receives automated POST requests. Slack incoming webhooks accept JSON payloads and post them as messages in a channel. If the webhook URL is rotated or revoked, requests return 403 Forbidden.
glossary.Lambda Environment Variable: A key-value pair stored alongside a Lambda function's configuration. The function code reads it at runtime. Changing the value requires updating the function configuration, not the code.

narrative_arc.call: A customer tweets about stale dashboard data at 10:52 AM. The ops lead sees it while scrolling his phone during standup. No alert ever arrived.
narrative_arc.threshold: The CloudWatch alarm has been in ALARM state for 47 minutes. It fired correctly. The SNS topic received the message. The ops lead opens the SNS console and crosses into the notification pipeline -- the part of the system nobody thought to monitor.
narrative_arc.trials: Three subscriptions exist on the topic. The email subscription shows PendingConfirmation. The Lambda subscription invoked successfully but the function returned an error. The SMS subscription shows a spending limit failure. Each path looks different. Each path is broken.
narrative_arc.revelation: Every notification path failed independently. The email was never confirmed. The Slack webhook was rotated eight days ago. The SMS budget was exhausted by a noisy alarm last week. Three redundant paths, three silent failures. The alarm did its job. The last mile failed in three different ways.
narrative_arc.return: The email subscription is confirmed. The Slack webhook URL is updated. The SMS spending limit is increased. A new CloudWatch alarm on NumberOfNotificationsFailed is created and routed to a separate, verified path. The notification pipeline now monitors itself.

components.name: CloudWatch Alarm (clarabridge-ingest-error-rate)
components.role: Monitors the Lambda ingest processor error rate and publishes to the SNS topic when the 5% threshold is crossed
components.failure_impact: If the alarm is misconfigured or disabled, no notification is triggered regardless of how many errors occur

components.name: SNS Topic (clarabridge-ops-alerts)
components.role: Receives alarm notifications and fans out delivery attempts to all subscriptions on the topic
components.failure_impact: If the topic policy blocks the CloudWatch service principal, the publish itself fails and no subscription receives anything

components.name: Email Subscription (ops-team@clarabridge.io)
components.role: Delivers alarm notifications to the ops team email distribution list
components.failure_impact: In PendingConfirmation status, SNS silently skips delivery -- no error metric, no log entry, no retry

components.name: Lambda Subscription (clarabridge-slack-notifier)
components.role: Receives the SNS message and posts it to the ops Slack channel via an incoming webhook URL stored in an environment variable
components.failure_impact: If the webhook URL is invalid, the function returns an error. Without a dead-letter queue, the failure is only visible in CloudWatch Logs for the function

components.name: SMS Subscription (+1-512-555-0147)
components.role: Sends a text message to the ops lead's phone as a last-resort notification path
components.failure_impact: When the account SMS spending limit is reached, delivery silently stops for the rest of the month with no notification to the account owner

components.name: Lambda Function (clarabridge-ingest-processor)
components.role: Processes 14 million events per day and writes to DynamoDB tables that feed customer dashboards
components.failure_impact: When this function fails, customer dashboards show stale data -- the upstream problem that triggered the alarm in the first place

system_narration.data_flow: The Lambda ingest processor writes events to DynamoDB. When it starts failing, the CloudWatch alarm detects the error rate crossing 5% and publishes a notification to the SNS topic clarabridge-ops-alerts. SNS fans out three delivery attempts: email to ops-team@clarabridge.io, Lambda invocation of clarabridge-slack-notifier (which POSTs to a Slack webhook), and SMS to the ops lead's phone number. Each path is independent.
system_narration.what_broke: All three delivery paths failed independently. The email subscription was in PendingConfirmation status since March 5 -- the confirmation email landed in the shared inbox's spam folder and was never opened. The Slack webhook URL was rotated on March 18 during a security audit, but nobody updated the Lambda environment variable, so every invocation returned 403 Forbidden. The SMS spending limit ($1.00 default) was exhausted on March 19 by a noisy alarm, silently stopping all SMS delivery for the rest of the month. When the alarm fired on March 26, zero humans were notified.

hints.text: The CloudWatch alarm fired correctly. What did it send the notification to?
Check the SNS topic subscriptions. What is the status of each one?
There are three subscriptions. One is PendingConfirmation. What about the other two -- are they actually delivering?
All three paths are broken: email is PendingConfirmation, the Slack Lambda is getting 403 (rotated webhook), and SMS hit the $1.00 default spending limit.

Fix: confirm the email subscription, update the Slack webhook URL in the Lambda environment variables, increase the SNS SMS spending limit, and add a subscription filter that sends to a confirmed backup channel.

resolution.root_cause: All three SNS notification paths were broken: the email subscription was PendingConfirmation for three weeks (confirmation email went to spam), the Slack webhook Lambda was receiving 403 Forbidden (webhook URL rotated), and SMS delivery silently failed due to the $1.00 default account spending limit -- so when the CloudWatch alarm fired, zero humans were notified.

fix_criteria.description: Player identifies that the email subscription is in PendingConfirmation status
Player identifies that all three notification paths (email, Slack Lambda, SMS) are broken
Player proposes confirming the email subscription and fixing the other delivery paths
Player proposes monitoring the notification pipeline itself (delivery failures, subscription health)

learning_objectives: SNS email subscriptions require confirmation -- PendingConfirmation subscriptions silently drop messages
SNS SMS delivery has a default account spending limit of $1.00/month that silently drops messages when exceeded
Notification pipelines need monitoring: SNS delivery metrics (NumberOfNotificationsFailed) should trigger a separate alert path
sop_steps: 1. Acknowledge the incident and join the Clarabridge incident channel. Note that the team learned about the outage from a customer tweet, not from any alerting system.
2. Confirm the CloudWatch alarm clarabridge-ingest-error-rate is in ALARM state using describe-alarms. Verify the alarm action targets SNS topic clarabridge-ops-alerts.
3. List subscriptions on the topic using list-subscriptions-by-topic. Record the protocol, endpoint, and SubscriptionArn for each subscription.
4. Identify that the email subscription to ops-team@clarabridge.io has SubscriptionArn set to PendingConfirmation -- delivery was never attempted.
5. Check CloudWatch Logs for the clarabridge-slack-notifier Lambda function. Identify the 403 Forbidden errors from the Slack webhook API indicating a rotated webhook URL.
6. Check SNS SMS account attributes using get-sms-attributes. Confirm the MonthlySpendLimit is $1.00 and the SMSMonthToDateSpentUSD metric shows the limit is exhausted.
7. Confirm the email subscription by locating the confirmation email in the ops-team@clarabridge.io spam folder and clicking the confirmation link. If expired, delete and re-create the subscription.
8. Update the clarabridge-slack-notifier Lambda environment variable SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL with the current webhook URL using update-function-configuration.
9. Increase the SNS SMS spending limit using set-sms-attributes with a MonthlySpendLimit of $50 or higher.
10. Configure a dead-letter queue on the clarabridge-slack-notifier Lambda to capture future invocation failures.
11. Create a new CloudWatch alarm on NumberOfNotificationsFailed for the clarabridge-ops-alerts topic. Route this alarm to a separate, verified notification path (e.g., a PagerDuty integration).
12. Run a periodic subscription health check (weekly cron or AWS Config rule) that alerts if any subscription on critical topics is in PendingConfirmation status.
13. Verify all three paths deliver a test notification. Document the incident timeline and remediation in the post-incident review.

related_failure_modes.scenario: SNS subscription filter policy silently drops messages
related_failure_modes.how_it_differs: The subscription is confirmed and the endpoint is healthy, but a filter policy on the subscription does not match the message attributes. SNS increments NumberOfNotificationsFilteredOut instead of NumberOfNotificationsFailed. The message is intentionally dropped, not failed, so delivery status logging does not capture it as an error.
related_failure_modes.prevention: Audit subscription filter policies when changing message attribute schemas. Monitor the NumberOfNotificationsFilteredOut metric alongside NumberOfNotificationsFailed to detect unexpected filtering.

related_failure_modes.scenario: SNS topic policy blocks the publishing service
related_failure_modes.how_it_differs: The publish itself fails instead of the delivery. If the topic policy does not grant sns:Publish permission to the cloudwatch.amazonaws.com service principal, the CloudWatch alarm action returns AccessDenied and no message reaches the topic at all. The alarm shows ActionsExecutionState as FAILED.
related_failure_modes.prevention: Include a Condition with ArnLike on aws:SourceArn in the topic policy to allow CloudWatch alarms. Test alarm actions in staging by manually setting the alarm state with set-alarm-state.

related_failure_modes.scenario: Lambda destination or DLQ misconfigured, failures vanish
related_failure_modes.how_it_differs: The Lambda subscription is confirmed and SNS invokes the function successfully, but the function fails internally. Without a dead-letter queue or on-failure destination, the failed invocation is retried and then discarded. The failure is only visible in CloudWatch Logs for the function, which nobody monitors.
related_failure_modes.prevention: Always configure a dead-letter queue or on-failure destination on Lambda functions triggered by SNS. Set a CloudWatch alarm on the DLQ ApproximateNumberOfMessagesVisible metric.

related_failure_modes.scenario: SNS delivery retry exhaustion for HTTPS endpoints
related_failure_modes.how_it_differs: An HTTPS subscription endpoint is temporarily unavailable. SNS retries according to the delivery policy (default: 3 retries over 20 seconds for HTTP). If all retries fail, the message is discarded unless a DLQ is configured on the subscription itself. Unlike Lambda, SNS subscriptions support their own redrive policy.
related_failure_modes.prevention: Configure a subscription-level redrive policy pointing to an SQS dead-letter queue. Monitor the NumberOfNotificationsFailed metric and the DLQ depth.

sop_practices: After creating any SNS email subscription, immediately verify it reaches Confirmed status -- do not assume the confirmation email was received. Check spam folders and set a calendar reminder to verify within 24 hours.
Set the SNS SMS MonthlySpendLimit to a value that accommodates peak alert volume with headroom, and create a CloudWatch alarm on SMSMonthToDateSpentUSD that fires at 80% of the limit.
Configure dead-letter queues on every Lambda function invoked by SNS, and on every HTTPS subscription, so that delivery failures are captured rather than silently discarded.
Monitor the notification pipeline itself: create a CloudWatch alarm on NumberOfNotificationsFailed for every critical SNS topic and route it to a separate, independently verified notification path.
